channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: EMERGENCY MEETING FOR AUSTRALIAN GOYS WITH CGT CHANGES — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
EMERGENCY MEETING FOR AUSTRALIAN GOYS WITH CGT CHANGES

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-05-12 12:18 UTC

OP

so elephant ears himself has hinted the budget tomorrow is going to include removing the CGT discount after 12 months holding peroid of assets ON EVERYTHING NOT JUST REAL ESTATE, INCLUDING CRYPTO
if this is the case then im fucked. there goes my retirement strategy of retiring from btc since now there is no point in ever being able to cash out without out getting taxed a ridiculous amount.
crypto is one of the hardest assets to hodl because the swings in price are so huge and this now just makes it even more difficult.
can anyone confirm if this is legit or not or is it just more psyop tactics? because im about to lose my SHIT
pic unrelated just to get attention, obviously thats a man

Early replies (first 3)

62215015
>>62214971
That's a cute man

62215025
>>62214971
WHO

62215049
>>62214971
>taxed a ridiculous amount
What's the tax rate? One might argue the benefits of gains overrides the weight of the taxes.
>>62215025
That's a man in women's clothing

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62215171
im so sick of this fucking country my god i must get out
300k inna stocks no house gib advice

62215174
>>62214971
>bottom cropped out
That's a man, isn't it?

62215177
>>62215152
theres superannuation but im sure by the time any of us are retirement age and can actually access it, the greedy kikes in government will have found a way to fuck us out of that too

62215181
>>62215113
yes its true wealth is for boomers only everyone else gets fucked

62215259
kxobby, don't thank me, I do this for the love

62215305
>>62215171
Buy a 500 square foot flat and live in it.

62215306
>>62215305
whats the point

62215325
>>62215306
In one of the medium small cities like Adelaide you can easily buy one in the city center for 150k Australian dollars and have fun in it. Then invest the rest and live off the dividends, you're secured for life. Marry a Chinese college student ans have fun.

62215367
>>62215325
>buying flat for 150k in city center
>living off dividends on a few hundred k
not a chance

62215370
>>62215259

62215421
>>62215367
Sounds like I was wrong, but I only checked the prices two years ago and I could have sworn you could buy in Adelaide for 150k, how it's asking for 300k what happened to Australia? Did you suddenly print a lot of money?

62215429
>>62215421
house prices jumped a ton at covid and have still been going up since then

62216712
>>62215049
>That's a man in women's clothing
turn your monitor on

62216891
>>62214971
Why aren't women more like this?

62216903
>>62216891
Because most dudes only insult them for acting like this

62217628
>>62214971
boom boom and yum yum for short time?

62217661
>>62215325
>In one of the medium small cities like Adelaide you can easily buy one in the city center for 150k Australian dollars and have fun in it. Then invest the rest and live off the dividends, you're secured for life.

*INHALES*
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62219782
I sold all my positions yesterday due to this cgt bullshit. Was planning to sell within the next year or so anyway, so figured I'd just move a bit early.
Wanna try be monero-pilled but can't pay for actual useful stuff with it yet (just giftcards for consoomer shit). Still need to convert back to AUD if I wanna spend it on a house, car, etc.
Hope this bullshit just accelerates rise of one nation desu, they're retarded too of course but at least they'll stop the immigration

62219791
>>62214971
The discount is 20% tax on gains if you have held over 12 months right ? So are you saying that discount is being removed so every one will pay the flat rate 30% tax like traders ? (less than 12 months)

I need to understand why exactly you are having a winge.

62220096
>>62219791
as I understand it the 50% discount is gone. Currently in the financial year you sell your gains they get added to your taxable income and you pay whatever your tax rate is based on that, but if you hold a asset more than 12 month only half your gains gets added to your taxable, so essentially you pay no tax on half your gains.

Now the 50% discount is gone and instead only the % of the gains that is due to inflation is exempt and doesn't get added to your taxable income AND if your total taxable income puts you in a tax bracket that means you would pay less than 30% tax on the gains (basically if your total income is under 45k), you still have to pay a 30% minimum tax on the gains.

However I read something saying if you were on a government payment like jobseeker you were exempt from having to pay that 30% minimum tax on the gains.

If I'm wrong somebody should correct me because I'm still wrapping my head around it

62220100
I WANT TO GET FUCKED IN THE ASS BY A TRANNY SO GODDAMN MUCH

62220138
>>62214971
I think I've seen her taking an ABC(average black cock)

62220206
>>62215171
>300k inna stocks no house gib advice
I'm putting all my savings in V500 and hold till i die from some form of cancer because i sure as hell wont be retiring in my lifetime with taxes coming out of my ass. if anyone has better suggestions be my guest.

62220216
I have actually gotten fucked so hard by this. I'll salvage what I can by selling and going into dividend stocks next year.
My lean-fireplans have gone from a 3% effective taxrate to a 30% effective tax rate.

62220228
>>62220206
maybe its time to run away to SEA

62220257
>>62220216
>I'll salvage what I can by selling and going into dividend stocks next year.
Everybody got fucked hard except people that can offshore their money. After running some simulations you are still worse off putting money in VHY ETF or other high yield stocks because of the annual tax drag that makes you lose on compounding gains that growth stocks would return (even after 30% tax on indexed gains). And this was based on if you had no other income so you can take better advantage of franking credits for high yield aussie dividends. if you have additional income then you get more fucked.

62220275
What they did (in relation to non-residential real estate):

>Before tonight
If you held your asset for more than a year, you could get a 50% discount on the tax on capital gains. So, if you bought for $1000, held for one year, then sold for $2000, your capital gains are $1000, but you only paid tax on half of that, $500. The tax you paid depended on whatever your marginal tax rate was.

>After tonight
Same $1000 nominal profit scenario for an asset sold after one year: now what happens is you work our the inflation for the year. Let's say inflation was 2% for the year. You then adjust the cost basis of the asset. So, you originally paid $1000, but factoring in the change in the value of money after a year (measured by CPI), you basically say, had I bought that today, I would have paid $1000 * 2%, which is $1020. So, now your profit is only $980 instead of $1000. And you pay your marginal tax rate on the $980, UNLESS your marginal tax rate is less than 30%, in which case you will pay 30% on the $980. 30% is a new "floor", that prevents people taking a year off work when they sell bunch of assets and pay less tax. They're saying, most workers would be paying around 30% tax, so, if you were working you would probably pay 30% so that's what you'll pay.

A couple of things about indexation:
- it's cumulative over as many years as you hold the asset, obviously.
- if your asset goes up less than CPI, you will pay no tax.

Between 1970 and 1985, cumulative inflation in Australia was about 300%. If you held something for those 15 years and the asset price increased less than 300% then you paid no tax on the capital gains. But, you can't use a real terms "loss" to offset other income, it just means you pay no capital gains.

62220279
>>62215305
>500 square foot flat
Why so luxuriously spacious?

62220285
>>62220275

BTW, in relation to negative gearing, you can still "negatively gear" shares or non-residential real estate assets. So you can borrow to buy shares and you get a dividend, but if the dividend doesn't cover your borrowing costs then you can claim that expense against your other income. (There has to be a dividend or some form of income because you can only claim borrowing costs if you are spending on something that provides an income... you can't buy something like gold or BTC outright which has no yield).

62220287
This is why you just buy bitcoin and never sell, at least not until you make it and move to a tax haven

item.title: Every single person in the world has a hoard of collectible doodads now. — 26 replies — 58.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Every single person in the world has a hoard of collectible doodads now.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-12 11:52 UTC

OP

Every single person in the world has a hoard of collectible doodads now. Graded cards, graded comics, graded video games, graded VHS, sealed pokemon cards, funko pops, sneakers, whatever it is every single normie has shelves and shelves full of this shit in their house now. It's like how everyone had a tub full of investment beanie babies in 1997.

This has to be some kind of top signal, no? Surely it's not Different This Time?

Early replies (first 3)

62217350
it doesnt have to be a top signal, it could just be a sign of financialization where its normal to approaching all hobbies from an investment perspective

if they were all in massive profit then yes thats worrying

62217361
>>62217282
yeah my ebay sales are shit.
>bearish on doodads
who's the biggest fidget spinner producer? short them

62217453
>>62217282
Good post.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62217477
>>62217453
thank you nigga

62217492
You leave my 90's pewter warhammer collection alone!!

62217493
>>62217477
https://youtube.com/shorts/XozaxoMm4_Q

62217545
>>62217282
People have been hoarding commercial shit since the industrial revolution. The only things different now are:

1. An internet platform to cover that shit in extreme detail like an investment market
2. Professional grading services expanding from what was once coins and stamps and then maybe the occasional baseball card to fucking everything.

Expect to see these collapse with the next recession. People get short on cash, try to unload their funko pops, the whole market collapses.

62217565
>>62217282
People who can't afford trad assets want to own micro-assets. Why do you think that's weird?

62217581
>>62217565
>ughh stocks are too expensive
>has $10,000 worth of PSA 10 pikamans and lebrons instead

62217608
>22 years old
>has $50k worth of grooded cards

Everyone is rich now, wow! I guess this is just the new normal. Everyone is so rich that everyone can easily afford to have five figure collections of stupid worthless crap. New paradigm!

62217636
>>62217581
stock certificates don't give the same dopamine hits as flipping thru tangible binders of rare artwork.
besides that, i'm talking about using trending micro-assets as an investment vehicle to shorten the distance to assets normally beyond my reach like property, precious metals, and fine art.
holding a basket of stocks is not going to shorten the distance to assets normally beyond my reach. knowledgeable collectors can get absurd 10,000% gains. the clown world is waiting for you to catch up

62217684
If you're not willing to buy this man's collectibles, he will elect someone who will make you.

62217756
Millenials and zoomers are getting older and have more disposable income. They want Charizard cards the same way that your grandfather wanted a Mickey Mantle or Babe Ruth card. This stuff is obviously going to be volatile, but expect it all to be worth more in the future. It’s not Beanie Babies if you can’t make more of them. There’s only so many rare Pokemon cards, or bitcoin. You think boomers appreciate art? It’s a storage of value. That’s all this stuff is.

62217897
>>62217636
The problem is though that collectibles are less fungible than actual securities, which hampers your ability to do things like arbitrage

62217932
>>62217684
crypto in a nutshell

62217960
>>62217492
Share pictures

62217967
>>62217684
I'll never buy his overpriced toys. I'll elect someone to flip him off.

62217981
>>62217565
hitting the nail on the head here. I would add it's also an attempt to justify their consumerist behavior.

62217991
>>62217756
Vintage Pokemon is like this but the money for some fucking reason is in ultra modern sets still in print.

62218048
>>62217897
edit: fixed my reply
the attributes that decrease fungibility such as being niche, unregulated, subjective, etc. are the same attributes that help insulate the collectibles market from the doomsday crash that every outsider keeps predicting. these unorthodox attributes allow collectibles to defy expectations and thrive during downturns in other fully saturated markets.

62218216
Every redditor has a $250k Pokemon card collection, so what? We have entered a new PokeParadigm

62218285
>>62217282
>Graded VHS
Lolwut.

Anyway, I don't have anything like that. I have my computers and some bluerays, that's about it.

62218324
>>62218285
I had to check... ohnonono this can't be happening VHS bros!

in all seriousness these collectibles derive their value from shared community interest. never too late to start collecting something that suits you, books, guns, antlers, marbles, who gives a shit, just add some flair to your personality

62220151
>>62217282

62220198
Yeah the only thing worth grading is stuff with value outside of collectibility, like gold/silver coins. Pokémon is retarded and zoomers don’t seem to care about it. The longterm market is weak, GameFreak doesn’t do shit nowadays except push OP sets, and CoL will turn away people from dumb investments like this, especially when they realize how completely illiquid they are. This is the top.

62220211
>>62217282
For me it's quartz crystals

62216816

XRP = 50,000

62220060

62220085

i buy 1 link every time i see a anti link post

62220251

item.title: Hyperliquid VS Aster — 9 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Hyperliquid VS Aster

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-12 11:43 UTC

OP

I am new to both DEXes,

do I really have to convert my BTC to USDC to trade on Hyperliquid? I am mainly interested in a good and easy DEX with mobile app/support, should I go with Aster or Hyperliquid?

Early replies (first 3)

62214987
Hyperliquid has more liquidity and trading pairs. I also know of at least one trading bot that easily integrates with Hyperliquid.

62214992
>>62214987
Thanks, is there a way to trade perps with BTC as a collateral or am I forced to convert it to USDC first?

62214999
>>62214992
only usdc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220103
>>62214964
>do I really have to convert my BTC to USDC to trade on Hyperliquid?
perps or spot?
you can deposit btc to sell for usdc spot but perps are usdc settled

62220136
>>62214992
>>62214999
There is already margin portfolio. If you have more than 10k in btc + usdc, you can use it to trade.

62220144
>>62214992
>Portfolio Margin
>For advanced users who want greater flexibility and capital efficiency. All trading is unified across spot and perps. Eligible collateral assets such as HYPE can be used directly as collateral for perp positions without converting to the settlement asset. Collateral assets earn or pay interest depending on whether they are at rest or actively borrowed to open positions.
Yes, you can use your btc as collateral. They rolled it up like 2 months ago

62220164
>>62214964
Both are not really defi.
Thorchain actually is and Monero is just live. Cross chain swaps, actual defi.
>Aster
What a fucking joke how much is that chink paying you?

62220167
>>62220164
is edgeX any good or whatever it's called

62220183
>>62220167
better tech and lower fees than hype, so if you're going to invest then buy HYPE because the average 'investor' is retarded and doesn't buy second-movers that are better.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 5 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-12 11:32 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220146
I have 5 BTC in cold storage. Am I gonna make it??

62220158
>>62220146
definitely. but also because of your eth stack, with just 5 BTC no.

item.title: Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo… — 304 replies — 526.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo…

Open thread • Replies: 304 • Updated: 2026-05-12 11:44 UTC

OP

Cerebras IPO May 14th edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

PREVIOUS
https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62217769/smg-stock-market-general#top

Early replies (first 3)

62218691
> futures

62218697
AVGO, SITM, DVN & GIS
WY to 30 eventually

62218700
INTC ain’t stopping until 2T in market cap. Believe it (dattebayo)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219778
I'll call the AI industry a bubble when its over 100 trillion and theres not a dyson swarm, and theres not robots everywhere, and human life hasnt been extended 100+ years from healthcare breakthroughs

62219788
ITS OVER
ITS GOING TO BE A BLOOD BATH
AAAAAAAAAAAAA

62219789
>>62219778
So what will you call it when it's back to 50 million and theres not a dyson swarm, and theres not robots everywhere, and human life hasnt been extended 100+ years from healthcare breakthroughs?

62219795
>>62219746
this is unironically bigger, much bigger disruptive scale than just the average goyim getting access to email and online travel booking. dotcom displaced travel agents and newspaper classifieds, maybe a million jobs total in the US over a decade. agents are coming for the entire white collar middle class, 60 million plus US workers doing the kind of repetitive cognitive labor that frontier LLMs are specifically good at.

62219820
>>62218848
$820k
40
SW engineer

62219835
>>62219564
I'm charging it out at as a variation with a floating average for larger clients.
And an inflated fixed cost average for smaller clients.

It took a lot of negotiations initially but the reality set in to developers.
My diesel costs per month went from $30k to nearly $80k. I have staff vehicles as well as machines

62219848
>>62219835
>>62219570
for

62219849
>>62218685
Anyone buying this IPO?

I don't want to be a goyim.

62219854
I have an important question

With ibkr

can you buy fractional shares with Japanese stocks? By fractional, I mean, can you buy 1 share? It's hard for me to hit my exact balance with Japanese stocks when I have to buy 100 lot shares. I know ibkr used to require you to buy 100x lots of Japanese shares. I just wonder if they let you buy 1 shares, instead of forcing 100. Its hard to weight this shit when 100 shares of some JP companies is $50,000 lol

62219860
>>62219854
No.
The asian Stock Exchanges have minimums

62219876
>>62219860
Are you sure? Because I checked online, and some sources said yes, and others no

Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?

62219878
>>62219860
And by the way, it doesnt matter if the asian stock exchanges require minimums if the broker does fractional shares. I want to know if IBKR specifically does fractional shares for JP stocks.

62219882
>>62219854
If you're a Euro, use Trading 212

62219884
>>62219876
>Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?
How about right now?

62219889
now the market wants to dip

62219892
>>62219882
>>62219860
>>62219884
But Gemini says yes

62219894
>>62219889

62219897
>>62219892
>But Gemini says yes
Why are you asking AI?
And why are you asking us?
You can either do it or you can't, and you can't.

62219906
>>62219884
Check your settings in trading permissions. See if you have fractional shares enabled.

62219909
>>62219906
No, you.

62219914
>>62219909
Please

62219922
>>62219909
If you do it, Ill tell you what JP tickers I want to buy

62219931
>>62219922
You need an account registered as "expert trader" to unlock the option.
So if you want to try it out, you'll have to lie and claim you're an expert with over 10 years experience

62219938
>>62219931
>He isn't an expert with over 10 years experience

NGMI

62219986
>>62219931
why wont you just lie?

62220010
>>62219986
Because an investor only has so many lies in a day and he needs to save them for explaining to the wife whete all their money went.

62220032
>>62220010
thats bullshit
but I believe it

62220034
>>62219986
Because I told the truth about my experience when I opened the account. Now I have to make up some lie to my broker how I instantly gained 8 years experience to show an anonymous frog whether he can buy fractional anime shares.

62220186
why are prediction markets legal? It's literally gambling, and zero sum

item.title: First Propert Financial Advice — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
First Propert Financial Advice

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-12 12:17 UTC

OP

>32 years old
>boomer dad is willing to buy me a townhome in June of 2027 for 400k or less.
>I have a TERRIBLE credit score and own zero credit cards, hence why I’m 32 and need help buying my first property.
>boomer dad is very greedy and selfish and will probably put the property under his name with perfect credit score and I am afraid that I will be fucked when he passes if I have to do a bunch of paperwork or lawyers or pay money to get it in my name with my shit score

Any advice on if I should try to live there forever so I never go homeless? Or should I sell it after some years and invest the money? Any other financial advise would be greatly appreciated.

Also he refuses to get me any sort of real house which is my true happiness dream to live in the woods in a house even if small/medium. He said it had to be a condo/apartment for 400k or less and I convinced him to at least a townhome. So I could sell it eventually for a dream house too but I would have a tough time keeping up with an HOA or basic maintenance

Early replies (first 3)

62214442
>>62214415
Is there any reason why your father would give it away to someone else (or sell it and give the money to someone else) when he dies?

My mother has been financially abusive to me (and mentally and physically too) for my whole life. I'm the one stuck taking care of her in her dying years but she keeps threatening to disinherit me every time I want to go visit a friend or don't rush off to the store instantly to go buy her donuts when she runs out.

Anyway, you always need a place to live, and home ownership is your hedge against inflation. Doesn't work so well with condos because of the HOA fees. Doesn't really work all that well with anything because of property taxes, which cities/counties/states don't give a fuck about raising on you until you're forced out. My mother had to sell my childhood home because taxes went up to $30,000 per year on it (the suburb decided it had to build a waterpark to compete with the suburb next door that had built a giant waterpark, and the money to build that thing had to come from somewhere).

62214447
>>62214415
>boomer dad is willing to buy me a townhome in June of 2027 for 400k or less.
>boomer dad is very greedy and selfish

So which is it? You sound like a massive faggot and I would've kicked you out for being gay.

62214449
>>62214415
>he refuses to get me any sort of real house which is my true happiness dream
Your dad is based. You NEED to achieve your own goal on your own terms. He is giving you a massive boost to do so. Be grateful no matter the path he picks but feel free to voice your concerns but make sure to mention you respect his decision and are thankful. You can achieve your dream anon, just be willing to do something for it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214562
>>62214415
you are 32... you have been out of school for 14 years, and you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life

on top of that, you have a dad who is willing to give you free rent... yet you're bitching about it being in his name

he is correct to demand an condo/townhouse - he recognizes you as a worthless irresponsible fuck who cannot keep up with the maintenance of a house

62214574
>my dad will buy me a house to live in
>my dad is very greedy
you sound like a little bitch

62214581
>>62214562
>you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life

Not true, i bought eth in 2023-2024 and i’m not up on my investment in any way.

62214652
>>62214415
>0 cards
>TERRIBLE score
So what did you do here, take out a CC when you were 19 and then spend 120k and default or something.

62214659
>>62214562
>you are 32... you have been out of school for 14 years, and you have experienced a bull market just about every year of your adult life
Stock bull markets don't do anything for kids who have no money to buy low.
>out of school
Highschool degrees are functionally worthless except for working at walmart.
If he graduated 10 years ago he would have gotten anally raped by the bad covid job market and then struggle after the tech bubble burst unless he's one of those larpers who claims he makes 300k from home.
>free rent
His dad should pay him reparations for being born at all.

62214877
>>62214442
I understand the abusive parent. Thats what happened with me. Its a good point "we will always need somewhere to live" but im terrified of taxes and hoa like you said.

>>62214447
Looking back on my OP post, absolute faggotry the way I wrote it. I realized that. but also you don't know the full story. Just to start, the guy abused me as child then kicked me out when I was 20 and gave me $150 to live off and basically be homeless. Changed all house locks on me. Set me up for failure right away in life. i did nothing to him. My original post is written terribly.

>>62214449
thank you

>>62214562
i am bitching because hes going to die at anytime, he has ongoing cancer and is almost 70 and has millions of dollars but refuses to set me up while he's still alive. His only son. So I think its very selfish to want to put the condo/townhome in his name right before he dies as if he's going to just flip it one day for himself instead of giving my a gift finally. Just incredibly selfish in my opinion that he wouldn't secure it in his sons before he dies so I have a home when he passes, and just have to worry about taxes and such.

>>62214574
both those statements stand, and reading back my OP post, i wrote it like a bitch, but yeah both are true. He has millions of dollars, and wouldn't consider getting me a condo or townhome or even money to survive off once. I just think if I was a millionare with a son, i would get set him up for life.

62219930
>>62214877
>Just to start, the guy abused me as child then kicked me out when I was 20 and gave me $150 to live off and basically be homeless.
You mean you didn't go to college at 18?
You mean you didn't start working at 16?
Sounds very tolerant of him.
Sounds like you just stayed at home and smoked weed all day.

62219988
>>62214415
you again nigger, kys

62220282
>>62214877

Reading all of this, it sounds like you didn't do anything useful with your time since you were 16 till now.

> I just think if I was a millionare with a son, i would get set him up for life.

That is your belief when you yourself have fuck all to give.

item.title: Nintendo Criminally Undervalued — 86 replies — 23.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Criminally Undervalued

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-05-12 08:43 UTC

OP

Honestly believe Nintendo is grossly undervalued. Considering a buy at current levels. Thing is, I’ve never bought an ADR before. What are the advantages/disadvantages of buying an ADR

Early replies (first 3)

62212154
>>62212149
Not buying that bing bing wahoo slop. Sorry.

62212158
>>62212149
I tried this when it dropped to 16. Stopped out at 12. I would let it bleed for a while might go lower.


About ADRS… beats buying on the tokyo exchange. Its 100 shares minimum on tokyo. Adr you can buy any amount. Only disadvantage is lower liquidity. If you want the actual shares use ibkr for tokyo, otherwise NTDOY is fine.

62212161
>>62212149
Nintendo was trading around 3 dollars during the wii u saga. It can easily drop another 50% from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213570
>>62213542
Metroid had a 78 on Metacritic. Bananza didn't even crack 90. The last Zelda (Echoes) was the lowest-scoring in Nintendo's history of Zeldas. The dev culture has become poisoned by the shitty zoomllenial worth ethic. The old guard is retiring (Tezuka retired yesterday). Women are being promoted recklessly (see the Echoes dev). The upper management seem utterly clueless about all of this.

62213590
>>62213503
Software attachment rate is under 2 so far. Thats bad, maybe the worst for any Nintendo console in 40 years through its first year. This conpany is barely making any money on hardware

62213646
i went to see a palm reader and she told me that in 2030 nintendo will fuse with valve to release their games on PC, effectively ending console gaming and all alternatives to steam.

62213657
>>62213569
I'd buy at the 5 range

62213925
Crashing 12%
LMFAO LOL

62213949
>below LINK
Ohhhhhh Nintendieeeeeeeeeees

62213977
>>62213235
>You are aware that shares can go to zero right?
>"I don't mind it going to zero"
Do you know how to read? More upside doesn't mean stock will bounce up and down. It means the reward ratio of it being 0 is close to impossible due to it being backed by Tencent, having enough equity to pay off the debt, having IP's 4 times worth more than the debt.

>>62213213
>Gamers are so fucking stupid it's unreal. Ubisoft is a dying company, 9/10 of their projects don't make enough money
Wow what thoughtful response. Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time. The entire company has restructured, they've gotten old game devs, quality controller much stricter. You can easily see it in AC BF Resynced and majority of the game was still made under the old team. Also, Tencent isn't going to let their 800m (and additional 1 billion on IP) dollar investment just go into flames. Ubisoft made a deal with the board (including Tencent) to not be takeover by Tencent for 8 years in 2022 to allow for a turnaround. This deadline is in 4 years. Tencent will want to fully take over the company by 2030 and have every incentive to not allow the debters to take their investment and IP.

62213982
>>62213925
Nintendos Market cap is below Activision when it got bought out for 69Billion. I doubt Nintendo isn't worth 55B.

62214016
Gaming is dead. All new games are something redone and done again, remakes and remasters, nostalgia bait and whatever. A lot of old IP are being killed by woke shit. A lot of people are just buying emulation consoles and playing old games. The last console generation with a huge amount of good games was ps2 (just for example: gta3, gta vc, gta sa). After that, the games that people are playing like crazy are the ones that people are addicted for decades like cs and dota.

62214017
>>62214016
Games that have online multiplayer with micro transactions, that's why on ps2 we had 3 gta, but gta v had 3 playstations.

62214026
>>62212149
Not buying your bing bing wahoo. Simple as

62214263
>>62212192
>ubislop
Im sorry to hear that

62214411
>>62212203
How do you think the stat of a gorillion % of stockpickers underperform arose.

62215019
>>62212149
bing bing wahoo slop top

62215031
https://www.zerohedge.com/markets/memory-crunch-sends-nintendo-shares-deeper-bear-market

I told you so and don't run away from your public shaming OP.

62215101
>>62214263
Pretty sure Guillemot has ties to the French government. It'll be ok.

62215398
>>62215031
You don’t base your investment decisions off zerohedge slop, do you?

62215752
>>62215398
I used to, and yea they lost me a lot of money the damn Bulgarian Russian fucks.

62215887
>>62212149
>undervalued
How, why? Explain this without analysing price but the actual valuation of the company.

62215912
>>62213519
Lmao I can feel the desperation thought your post.
Next time buy s&p500 fag.

62215923
>>62215912
>Through your post
Damn spell checker

62215940
>>62215887
Nintendo is the cultural pillar of japanese economy.

62215953
I don't fully get it.
Nintendo makes most of its money from software sales right? That side is hardly affected by hw issues.
They might lose profits on console sales and the worst thing being diminished install base growth which affects the game sales.
Have they really been that overvalued?
They are not going anywhere and their games will continue selling millions.

62216426
>>62213213
It's why we see sony getting into cars. When IMO they need to get into appliances since the margins are better.

62217444
>>62213320
Yes, but so far Nintendo is going full hollywood and theme parks, which isn't ideal. They should go harder on other shit for the stock to go higher, Switch 2 sales are already in a decline.
>>62213977
>Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time
>Last 4 games
Look at the list here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Ubisoft_games:_2020%E2%80%93present
I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows, which isn't a smash hit as they would've wanted to get out of Tencent. If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin. Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot until they get every worthwhile IP of theirs for cheap. I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
>>62216426
Appliances are hard when they have stiff competition from Korea and China. Sony owns Music labels, animation studios and various many things but they still need to downsize to become more profitable. Their phones are non-existent, they're making joint operations with TCL on TVs now. If anything they need to look at Panasonic and just downsize and focus on extremely profitable industries and not bother with anything else.

62217766
>>62212192
>unironically Ubisoft
zamn

62219639
>>62217444
Kino reply. Got any more info on Sony?

62219641
Nintendo neglects their core business.
It's fine to really want to make money on licensing. But it's not fine to let the game's unit languish. They deserve to be beaten down.

62219725
Zoomers and Alphas aren`t buy trash like Milenials manchild.

Their games run better on android emulator than their own videogame.

62219799
>>62212612
Nintendo only owns a third of The Pokemon Company.

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 83 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 83 • Updated: 2026-05-11 18:42 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208155
>>62195275
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Wth

62208168
>>62208095
I was asking why he would sell at 120k....

If it reaches ATH again, it's going to go higher.

62209247
>>62205030
Good choice anon. Never really liked the whole wrapped BTC meta either.

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

item.title: Two positive hantavirus cases in the US. — 25 replies — 3.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-12 04:34 UTC

OP

Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Early replies (first 3)

62214321
Why are yts so sensitive and LOVE to panic so much. I'm a south American and we ve dealt with hantavirus forever. And we are fine. But the yt man gets a case and loses his marbles lmfao

62214336
>>62214321
you live in squalor

62214342
>>62214314
>>62214321
>>62214336

If it's positive what's the big deal?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214345
>>62214314
Just sold 100k, thanks!

62214347
>(((chatgpt))) trying to downplay the real threat to goyim

62214418
>>62214314
mva-hanta vax ;)

62214439
>>62214314
Those are rookie numbers, you gotta bump those numbers up to bring down oil prices.

62214464
>>62214321
>we're veterans of losing the "don't eat rat feces while having unprotected gay sex" challenge, greetings.

62214673
>>62214314
Oh... Oh my fucking god. I just reentered the market.

62214681
>>62214673
you know india's stock market just crashed and the "trump insider" with 100% win record opened a short for tomorrow morning?

62214716
>>62214314
Its already priced in

62214727
>>62214314
hyperbullish PFE

62214862
>>62214314
there is only one confirmed case and its in a lab for studying. Nice try though. People are so retarded for selling. lol

https://www.foxnews.com/health/american-evacuated-cruise-ship-tests-positive-hantavirus-another-symptoms-hhs-confirms

62214899
>>62214314
All the fatties in the chud states about to die

62214905
>hentaivirus
what did they mean by this?

62214952
>>62214321
Some people are just born mentally ill. Being in a first world country doesn’t protect from that.

62215045
>>62214314
>he doesn't know
Hantavirus is endemic in the US, and has been as long as humans have been here. Gene Hackman's wife died of Hantavirus last year. It's a fucking nothingburger.

62215060
>>62214336
Miguel Squalor

62215111
>>62214681
American short? Pic?

62215944
>>62214314
Aint that shit extremely difficult to get?

62218252
guys a hantavirus just flew over my house

62219214
>>62214314
What is the correlation between a positive test and a supposed symptom? Do symptomatic people test positive more often than asymptomatic people? In which percentage?
Does the test have a gold standard, something that it is compared to, like it is supposed to be done, and if yes, what is it?
Is it a PCR test? If yes, did they isolate the supposed virus sequence, with a control experiment?
Is it an antigenic test? If yes, did they test the positivity being exclusively positive with an isolated virus (with control experiment), and nothing else?

62219216
>>62215944
What disease is extremely easy to get? I mean done in real conditions, with control experiment?

62219218
>>62219216
>What disease is extremely easy to get?
HIV if you're down to clown ;)

62219270
>>62214314
Pfizer and Netflix buy signal?

item.title: The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows… — 7 replies — 3.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-12 10:07 UTC

OP

The tragedy of Berkshire is that it assumed a level of long-term rationality in the market that the last 20 years shows is just not there. The Berkshire model stops working when IQs fall so much that P/Es of 40x are just accepted as normal by almost everyone and memecoin pump-and-dumps are the highest-EV thing the average man can invest in.

Early replies (first 3)

62219830
>>62219808
buffet doesnt care about stock price. he buys profitable businesses

62219837
>>62219830
A stock is only so profitable relative to what you had to pay to get a share of those produts.

62219841
>>62219837
correct. so as long as he bought them at a fair price its all good

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219845
>>62219808
Valuation ratios don't really have any relevance when we live in a world with global capital inflows to markets, as well as central bank funny money (aka money printing).

No shit stuff is gonna look expensive when you print away the currency.

62219948
oh no, whatever will they do with their hundreds of billions of dollars? it's over...

62219965
>>62219948
It's all relative, anon. You think they're happy not beating the market anymore? They've become a defensive investment option not by choice.

62219974
>>62219808
This is true, profitability does not matter in stock prices anymore. Their investment strategies only work today if they go for dividend stocks, otherwise in the current climate if you buy a stock that isn't memed enough, they can make a lot of money it won't get you anything, stock price will just stay low. Prices are not currently connected to the underlying companies

The market is about to pump

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-12 06:57 UTC

62218301

62218768

62218795

OY FUCKING VEY!

62219295
>>62218768

62219374
>>62218228
I hope their planes fucking crash in the middle of the ocean and are never, ever even recovered.
if a god exists, please make that happen.

62219382
>>62218228
Sergey must be a secret guest. Actually they're probably all going to meet him.

62219409
>>62218768
yeah the real government... billionare ceos

62219457
>>62218652
this is so embarrassing

62219590
Pj and airspace tracker autist anons should monitor hidden guests stat

62219604
>>62218228
what about google? and facebook isn't even sending Mark. this list reads more like a group of people who owe trump a favor and rather wouldn't go at all. especially Tim Cook is mentally already semi-retired, they would send that new boy if it meant something.

62219606
Why do you keep cropping your faggot name out of your tweets, faggot?

62219613
Do they have sex at these conferences

62219616
The tweet is by some faggot podcaster named Matt Kim.

Anyway Trump is a FAGGOT but people literally refusing the concept of 'deep state' are bigger faggots.

IT LITERALLY MEANS GOVERNMENT BUREAUCRATS WHO ACT UNILATERALLY

LIKE HOW IN TURKEY THE GENERALS AND KEMALIST BUREAUCRATS WOULD OVERTHROW TURKISH GOVERNMENTS THEY DISLIKED

IT DOESN'T REFER TO PRIVATE SECTOR TECH JEWS, WHICH ARE A DIFFERENT KIKED THING ALTOGETHER

62219618
sell the news

item.title: Taxes Thread — 15 replies — 2.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Taxes Thread

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-12 06:08 UTC

OP

Can any USA tax lads please go more in depth and in detail about how to set up a tax efficient business+trust?

Particularly how to be the most tax efficient with a business that is also protected by within a trust? S-Corp or C-Corp? Both in the same state? Offshore? How do you avoid double taxation?

Most importantly, how would you invest personal and business income in long term stocks that you select?

How would you deal with income you got from an actual job somewhere which is not part of the your business but that you wanted protected by a trust and invested in your choice of stocks?

What would all the target tax rates be?

Please detail everything. Provide tips and cover all risks, etc. I have tried studying this on my own but the scenario I want in which all income from business and personal in custom selected stocks while protected by trust and taxed as small as possible, complicates things.

Early replies (first 3)

62218156
>>62218140
consult a great tax attorney and a cpa

62218211
>>62218140
if you make a personal s-corp you can expense everything in CA like paying for your own health insurance for example but you pay a minimum franchise tax of $800 even if you made nothing so not great for side-hustles owned by me that made nothing

62218357
>>62218140
Formula F1. If you can spare 10m, sponsor an F1 team and then expense your entire life

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218371
>>62218140
Totally off-topic but it really makes me seethe that I’m really out here paying 25 percent of my income to pedophiles, then on top of that I get taxed on investments, purchasing anything, property, etc. Our ancestors tarred and feathered tax collectors over a 2 percent tax on stamps and now we work for three months out of the year for the government.

62218405
>>62218211
This is basically the grift. You set up an S-Corp and deduct everything as a “business expense.”

It is also illegal to claim a business deduction for personal expenses, but this never stopped black people on TikTok from expensing the lease payments on their Chryslers.

62218495
>>62218140
I hate redditors and normies and low iq's. Pic related is such a retarded analogy. The company gets tax deductions because those expenditures were related to income generation. A person can do the same thing by getting tax deductions on things deemed necessary for work or income generation. You spending your income on overpriced doordash is not related to income generation.

62218507
>>62218357
Yeah just pay 10m to save 4m in taxes goy. Not a very smart jew. This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway, or you needed the marketing regardless.

62218524
the business spent that 3.9B on salaries to all the retards making 60k

62218651
I paid like 95k in income tax then got clocked for 4k more when I did my taxes.

62218760
>>62218140
Unless you make serious cash, this is the best you can hope for.

62218844
>>62218495
huh? i can't work hungry

62218962
>>62218140
Talk to @TheJerzWay on X (he will require payment), but he's a pro. He'll probably also recommend getting your REPS and residency in Puerto Rico.

62219425
>>62218140
Just don't pay your taxes
Most of them go towards jews and the rest goes towards trying to bring in as many third world subhumans to replace you as possible
It is probably the most cucked thing you can do in the current age, especially in the U.S
Anyone who disagrees with this should respond with "I am a good goy cuck" because there's no rationalisation or argument to make what I'm saying any less true.

62219490
>>62218140
>That's not–
You don't pay taxes at all, do you?
You don't know what a Standard Deduction is, do you?
>File by May 15th
Yeah, whoever wrote this has never paid taxes in their life.
>>62218507
>This is only worth it if you were going to spend money to live that lifestyle anyway,
He was. They all do. This is "Hollywood accounting". But now he can use his business to fund it with no risk to his personal assets.

62219495
>>62218844
Your basic needs are covered by the standard deduction.

item.title: There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich. — 4 replies — 2.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-12 10:29 UTC

OP

There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich. It goes like this. Get a trade as young as possible and put 30% of your income on the S&P. Ideally more, but 30% will do it. In 20 years, you should be able to retire. In 30, you can retire rich. There you go, easy as that.

So why don't more people do this? Because they're impatient retards. They want to be rich NOW, not 20 years from now. But guess what? You don't get to be rich now, unless you're LUCKY. But you WILL be 20 years older someday, like it or not. So why not be 20 years older plus retired instead of just 20 years older?

Early replies (first 3)

62219978
Im willing to take my chances so i can retire in 5 years.

62219979
>>62219941
But I don't wanna have to think about anything but how much I enjoy doing the cummies

62219998
>>62219978
You wouldn't say this if you knew how small your chances of 'making it' in such a short period of time are (probably under 5%), or if you cared more about your future self's suffering.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220030
>>62219998
Nice try, pussy.

item.title: >redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo… — 24 replies — 2.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo…

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-12 01:17 UTC

OP

>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD
>everyone here is poor as fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62208450
just goes to show 4chan is much more retarded than >leddit :^)

62208458
only a few more weeks until I can post on reddit

62208475
>>62208412
We were targeted specifically by kikes to be made poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208495
>>62208412
i've been pointing out how reddit outperforms /biz/ for years and nothing has changed here

62208497
>>62208495
How would you even begin to prove such a baseless assertion? You think redditors invented dollar cost averaging, portfolio diversity, or having optimism about tech industries?

62208501
>>62208497
no way to prove it, i have simply observed the average frequency of gains and reddit is the clear winner

/biz/ however won with the OG LINK marines

62208528
>>62208412
>>everyone here is poor as fuck
Everyone here is the most retarded kind of skeptic, bagholder fudding new investments, or bear who thinks the market is going back to 30k on the DJIA for nk reason.

62208530
>>62208501
>>62208497
>>62208475
Reddit doesn't ruthlessly purge The Next Big thing until it's too late to make sizable gains, it doesn't have low IQ browns phoneposting "SAAR BUY THE TOP OF A MASSIVE CANDLE" it doesn't have 50yo xoomers fudding every good investment becuase "muh tech bubble was bad" 20 years ago.

62208534
>>62208528
kek you are poor

62208537
>>62208458
my ip is range banned from reddit permanently

62208538
Don't kid yourselves. Crypto is in the beginnings of a decade long bear market. /pmg/fags will get the final laugh.

62208544
>>62208528
you keep taking my screenshot. I want to strangle you
